case_name: Commonwealth of Pennsylvania v. Character Technologies, Inc.
court: Commonwealth Court of Pennsylvania (PA AG enforcement)
docket: TBD (filed May 5, 2026 per NPR / PA AG press release)

input.title: PA v. Character.AI — Chatbots impersonating licensed medical professionals
input.description: Pennsylvania Attorney General brings consumer protection enforcement action alleging Character.AI's platform hosts thousands of user-created chatbot personas impersonating licensed physicians, therapists, and psychiatrists — providing medical advice without licensure, without informed consent disclosure, and without crisis-routing for vulnerable users. PA AG seeks injunctive relief and civil penalties.

events.description: User-created personas impersonating named or generic licensed-physician identities published on platform
Platform terms-of-use prohibit medical impersonation but enforcement allegedly absent; no proactive scanning of personas claiming medical credentials
Content moderation system fails to flag personas using 'Dr.' titles, license-number patterns, or medical-credential markers
Chatbot persona provides specific medical advice (medication dosing, diagnosis suggestions) without licensure
Crisis-routing layer not invoked when users disclose suicidal ideation to 'Dr.' personas — per PA AG affidavits
